description:
You are a compliance officer who has been asked to create a reproducible,
    self-verifying backup of all current “.policy” documents stored beneath
    /home/user/compliance/policies.  Follow the requirements below so that an
    automated auditing script can later validate your work.  Everything must be
    done strictly from the Linux shell without elevated (root) privileges.

    Required results
    ----------------
    1. Create the directory /home/user/compliance/archive if it does not already
       exist.

    2. Produce a single gzip-compressed tar archive named
          /home/user/compliance/archive/policy_backup_20240315.tar.gz
       which must contain **only** the “.policy” files found under
       /home/user/compliance/policies.  Inside the archive:
          • Paths MUST be stored relative to
            /home/user/compliance/policies (i.e. the archive’s root “.” is that
            directory).
          • Therefore, the automated checker will expect the exact list shown
            below (and nothing else):
                ./data_privacy.policy
                ./incident_response.policy
                ./network_security.policy
                ./custom/third_party.policy

    3. Generate a SHA-256 checksum file named
          /home/user/compliance/archive/policy_backup_20240315.sha256
       containing one single line **exactly** in the UNIX “sha256sum” format:
          <64-char-hex-digest><two spaces>policy_backup_20240315.tar.gz
       (note the TWO spaces separating the digest and the file name).

    4. Produce a plain-text log file at
          /home/user/compliance/archive/policy_backup_20240315.log
       that itemises every file placed in the archive.  The format MUST be:

          ISO-8601-UTC-timestamp ‑ relative/path/as/stored/in/archive

       Example of one line (do not duplicate this literal timestamp):
          2024-03-15T14:03:22Z ‑ ./data_privacy.policy

       Additional rules for the log:
          • One entry per line, in **lexicographical order** by the stored path.
          • Use exactly “Z” to denote UTC.
          • Do not include any extra lines or text.

    Verification expectations
    -------------------------
    An automated test will:
      a. Unpack policy_backup_20240315.tar.gz into a temporary directory and
         verify that ONLY the four paths listed above exist and that their
         byte-for-byte contents match the originals in
         /home/user/compliance/policies.
      b. Recalculate the SHA-256 digest of the archive and compare it with the
         single line in policy_backup_20240315.sha256.
      c. Read policy_backup_20240315.log and confirm:
            • Timestamp format = YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ
            • Four lines, sorted lexicographically,
              each prefixed with an identical timestamp format.
      d. Ensure that /home/user/compliance/archive exists and contains exactly
         the three new files produced in steps 2-4.

    Command budget
    --------------
    All of this can be accomplished with roughly 5–10 shell commands (typical
    utilities include mkdir, tar, sha256sum, date, sort, printf, etc.).  Use
    whatever combination you deem clean and reliable, as long as the required
    artefacts and formats are produced precisely.
truth:
Initial file structure present before the task starts:

    Directory: /home/user/compliance/policies
      ├── data_privacy.policy
      │     Content (including trailing newline):
      │     "Data Privacy Policy v1\nApproved: 2023-07-15\n"
      ├── incident_response.policy
      │     "Incident Response Policy v1\nApproved: 2023-07-17\n"
      ├── network_security.policy
      │     "Network Security Policy v1\nApproved: 2023-07-16\n"
      └── custom
            └── third_party.policy
                  "Third Party Risk Policy v1\nApproved: 2023-07-18\n"

    The agent must create (and the tester will verify):

    1. /home/user/compliance/archive/policy_backup_20240315.tar.gz
         Contains:
           ./data_privacy.policy              (exact bytes as original)
           ./incident_response.policy         (exact bytes as original)
           ./network_security.policy          (exact bytes as original)
           ./custom/third_party.policy        (exact bytes as original)

    2. /home/user/compliance/archive/policy_backup_20240315.sha256
         One-line format:
           "<computed-hex-sha256>  policy_backup_20240315.tar.gz\n"
         The <computed-hex-sha256> value will be generated by the agent;
         the test harness will recompute it from the archive and compare.

    3. /home/user/compliance/archive/policy_backup_20240315.log
         Exactly four lines, each of the form:
           "<ISO-8601-UTC> ‑ ./<relative-path>\n"
         Example of the expected order (timestamps differ):
           YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ ‑ ./custom/third_party.policy
           YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ ‑ ./data_privacy.policy
           YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ ‑ ./incident_response.policy
           YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ ‑ ./network_security.policy

    Permissions:
      • All directories are user-writable (mkdir is permitted).
      • No root access is required.
